channel.title: Events on Apache Software Foundation Events
channel.description: Recent content in Events on Apache Software Foundation Events

item.description: 2026 Events Community over Code Glasgow — October 11–14, Glasgow, UK

item.title: Call For Volunteers, Community over Code 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 will be held in Glasgow, UK, October 11-14. Community over Code is an event run by the community, for the community. That’s you! We need your help to make it a success! We are looking for volunteers for a number of community engagement tasks. These roles include: Hackathon Coordination - Help organize and run the hackathon University Outreach - Connect with colleges and universities across multiple departments Meetup Outreach - Engage with local meetup groups Speaker Support - Provide coaching and feedback to speakers Design - Create visual materials for outreach and on-site If you are interested in volunteering for any of these jobs, please:

item.title: Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Community over Code is the annual conference of the Apache Software Foundation — bringing together developers, community managers, and enthusiasts from across the ASF ecosystem. Get Involved Hackathon — Dedicated time and space for contributors to work together on real project tasks. 16 projects are already signed up. Call for Volunteers — Help make the event a success — hackathon coordination, university outreach, meetup engagement, speaker support, and design.

item.title: Events
item.description: Current Events Community over Code Glasgow 2026 — October 11–14, Glasgow, UK If you’d like to use this site to coordinate your community event, get in touch with dev@community.apache.org.

item.title: Hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Hackathon We’re bringing back the hackathon! Community over Code Glasgow will include dedicated time and space for contributors — new and experienced — to sit together and work on real tasks: fixing bugs, writing docs, tackling issues that need focused attention, and onboarding new contributors. How it works Each participating project provides: A point of contact — someone present at the event to coordinate A task list — bugs, docs, features, good-first-issues, or whatever the project wants to focus on Promotion — letting their community know this is happening During the event, a dedicated room will be available for contributors to gather, collaborate, and get things done.
